name: Torallola, Rivert, and Salàs de Pallars Trail
name_original: Salàs de Pallars y Ermita de Sant Salvador de Toralla — Circular por el Pallars Jussà desde La Pobla de Segur
description: This difficult 25.7-mile mountain biking trail near Salàs de Pallars offers panoramic views and passes through charming villages.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around La Pobla De Segur are situated in the Lleida Pre-Pyrenees, offering a diverse landscape for riding. The region features a wide altitudinal range, from agricultural plains along the Noguera Pallaresa River to higher mountain areas. This varied terrain includes extensive trail networks, forests, and ridges, providing a range of environments for mountain biking. Key natural features include the Sant Antoni Reservoir and the Collegats Mountains, which contribute to the area's scenic routes. Best mountain bike trails in La Pobla De Segur The most popular mountain bike trail is Torallola, Rivert, and Salàs de Pallars Trail, a 25.7 miles (41.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers varied terrain through the Pre-Pyrenees. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Claverol Castle – Sossís Village loop from la Pobla de Segur, a difficult 29.9 miles (48.1 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and views of historical sites. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Pantano de San Antonio – La Coma Trail loop from Sant Pere de les Maleses, a 33.2 miles (53.4 km) trail leading through the Sant Antoni Reservoir area, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around La Pobla De Segur is defined by the Pre-Pyrenees mountains, the Noguera Pallaresa River, and the Sant Antoni Reservoir. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in La Pobla De Segur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Pobla De Segur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Pobla de Segur?
A: La Pobla de Segur offers a wide network of mountain bike trails. There are over 25 routes available, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Pobla de Segur?
A: The Lleida Pre-Pyrenees region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, especially at lower altitudes, while winter might bring colder weather to higher mountain areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails around La Pobla de Segur?
A: The terrain is highly diverse, ranging from agricultural plains along the Noguera Pallaresa River to higher mountain peaks. You'll encounter extensive trail networks, forests, ridges, and rocky areas. Trails like "Zona Bloc" feature stones and red earth, while others like "Madeleine" are crafted within forests. The area is known for its dynamic descents and varied environments.

Q: Are there trails suitable for beginners or families in La Pobla de Segur?
A: Yes, La Pobla de Segur offers trails for all skill levels. There are 6 easy routes and 11 moderate routes, providing options for less experienced riders or families looking for a more relaxed experience. The "Trenkabikers Territory" project focuses on creating trails for all skill levels, including gentle paths.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, La Pobla de Segur has 9 difficult routes. You might enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17547163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pobla de Segur loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 34.9 km with significant elevation gain. Other demanding trails in the region include the red and black trails in the De Monsor area, such as "Monsor," "Queralt," "Gramuntill," and "American Line," which require attention and skill.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in La Pobla de Segur are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pobla de Segur – Claverol Castle loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 39.3 km route that offers a circular experience.

Q: What natural landmarks or scenic views can I see while mountain biking in La Pobla de Segur?
A: Many trails offer spectacular views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2673111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Antoni Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You can also enjoy vistas of the Collegats Mountains and the Boumort Natural Reserve from various viewpoints, such as Santa Magdalena and Sant Miquel. The "Canarill Trail" and "Serengueti Trail" are particularly known for their aerial views of the reservoir.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Some routes might pass near the medieval old town of La Pobla de Segur, with its ancient gates and stately houses. You could also explore sites like the Old Oil Mill (1904), the Flour Mill, or the Romanesque Shrine of Sant Miquel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Claverol Castle – Sossís Village loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes views of historical sites.

Q: Is there dedicated support for cyclists in La Pobla de Segur?
A: Yes, La Pobla de Segur is committed to active tourism. The Tourist Office provides a trekking and cycling reception center equipped with showers and lockers for cyclists. Additionally, the area features a 1,000m² pumptrack for various non-motorized sports.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in La Pobla de Segur?
A: While specific trailside parking isn't detailed, La Pobla de Segur is a town with general parking facilities. It's advisable to check local signage or the town's official website for public parking areas suitable for accessing the trail networks.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in La Pobla de Segur?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views of the Sant Antoni Reservoir and surrounding mountains, and the extensive network of trails that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any hidden gem trails or less-known routes worth exploring?
A: While many trails are well-known, the "Trenkabikers Territory" constantly develops and maintains new routes. Trails like "Orugues" and "SuperMario" are known for their fun and dynamic descents. Exploring the diverse sections like "Zona Bloc" or the forest trails of "Mascarell" and "Madeleine" can offer unique experiences away from the most frequented paths.
tips:
Beautiful little town with a lot of charm, where we find a popular collector's museum. With prior reservation, we will enjoy a guided tour of the stores set in the 50s, 60s and 70s that will transport us to childhood if we are already adults.
We can also see the Church of Nuestra Señora del Coll

Demanding route that passes through the pretty town of Rivert and the highest point is the hermitage of San Salvador. From this point it is a descent along a technical path to the town of Toralla. The second path that we are going to look for first is uphill but you can pedal all the way to finally enjoy the descent to near the town of La Pobla de Segur. We only have to return to Salàs along the beautiful path that runs near the lake.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult, often described as a 'Black' difficulty route. It requires very good fitness due to its length, significant elevation gain, and varied terrain, including some challenging climbs and technical sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that starts and ends in La Pobla de Segur. You can typically find parking in or around the town to begin your ride. It's part of the well-signposted network from the El Pallars Jussà MTB Centre.

faqs.question: Is the Torallola, Rivert, and Salàs de Pallars Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular mountain biking route, meaning you will finish your ride back at your starting point, typically La Pobla de Segur.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll ride on rural paths, through cropland and forests, and encounter both climbs and descents. Some segments may be unpaved and difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike. The broader region is known for rocky ridgelines, forest singletracks, technical switchbacks, and ancient stone paths.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Spanish Pyrenees region offers great mountain biking, but specific conditions vary. Autumn is notable for the Salàs forest being rich in mushrooms, suggesting pleasant conditions. Generally, spring and autumn offer cooler temperatures and beautiful scenery, while summer can be hot at lower elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers magnificent panoramic views of the Sant Antoni reservoir, especially from the village of Sant Joan de Vinyafrescal and near the Sant Martí settlement. You'll also pass through picturesque towns like Rivert and Salàs de Pallars Historic Village, and near the Hermitage of San Salvador de Toralla.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other mountain biking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Torallola-Ribert-Salàs de Pallars, Salàs de Pallars, Barranc dels Lleons, El bosc d'Erinyà, and Serrat de Sant Roc trails, among others.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Torallola, Rivert, and Salàs de Pallars Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not provided, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to always clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to ride this specific trail. It is part of a larger network of trails from the El Pallars Jussà MTB Centre, which are generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and the varied terrain, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), and navigation tools. Weather can change in the Pyrenees, so layers of clothing are recommended. Consider a first-aid kit as well.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through several villages such as Sant Joan de Vinyafrescal, Rivert, and Salàs de Pallars. You may find small shops or cafes in these towns, particularly in Salàs de Pallars, which could offer opportunities to refuel or grab a drink.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of San Salvador de Toralla
